old_claim.note: Timber framing was treated as an enabling capability for coal mining without direct edge-level source support.

new_claim_absent: No direct timber-framing dependency remains because the node is scoped to coal extraction generally, not to a particular mine-support construction technique.

source_shape.source_locator: Coal article: Britannica describes coal as an extractive and industrial fuel topic without making timber framing a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports the coal-mining node through coal extraction and industrial use, not mine timbering as a required prior technology.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Timber supports are an implementation detail in some underground mines; direct graph edges should represent the broader extractive capability.

ontology_before: Coal Mining depended directly on Timber Framing, implying a building-frame technology was required for the coal-mining node.
ontology_after: Coal Mining keeps Mining as its direct prerequisite and removes timber framing as overly specific infrastructure context.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: timber_framing is no longer a direct prerequisite for coal_mining.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to timber-supported underground shaft mining.
A stronger source showed timber framing directly enabled the earliest scoped coal extraction.
A dedicated underground_coal_mine_supports node were added and required this construction lineage.
short_reason: Timber framing is mine-support context, not the broad extraction prerequisite.
